last_item: Migrate — the single-db overwrite gate is split across three intent-shifts (checkbox → 'Continue…' button → type-the-name modal) with no inline warning when overwrite is checked (med/M). Proposed fix: a conditional inline danger Callout when overwrite is armed.
last_result: REJECTED on restraint (the loop's 4th Mode-F reject; pattern of iters 5/6/7). Implemented the restraint-aware version: a conditional Callout tone=danger below the single-db overwrite checkbox, shown ONLY while checked, stating the target is dropped/recreated + 'you'll type its name to confirm next' (mirroring the cluster form's inline warning). Added a test (warning appears on arm, gone on disarm); 14 Migrate tests green. Panel: 3 SHIP (UX heuristics 'consequence at arm-time'; Sam 'the safety net I want when I tick the box'; Priya 'zero added clicks, informational') but the RESTRAINT CRITIC BLOCKED and is never overruled, with decisive code-level reasoning: the single-db overwrite is ALREADY a three-stage escalating gate — checkbox label says '(destructive)', button flips to 'Continue…', and the modal has its own danger Callout + a type-the-name confirm that is the real execute-time gate. The new Callout just restates the modal one click earlier (user reads 'dropped and recreated, can't be undone' twice in seconds) and even pre-narrates the confirm step. The 'match the cluster form' steelman is FALSE on inspection: ClusterForm's Callout is ALWAYS-ON and carries info its label omits ('drops every matching database'), a categorically scarier standing warning — copying its shape misses its reason. And the destructive action can't fire from this screen at all (the button only opens the modal), so 'visibility at arm-time' buys nothing executable. Reverted both files clean; recorded the lesson ('count the warnings already firing on the same interaction before adding another; don't justify a sibling-view copy by shape — verify it carries info the local label/modal lacks'). No code shipped, no gates needed (revert only). Next top item: DatabaseTuning — add one reassuring intro line (defaults already tuned for the hardware; the typical indie app needs no changes); intro-only change is S.
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn — rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres — minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
